page: Microsoft Azure E96as_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Easv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 96 vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB memory-to-vCPU ratio. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier (top 10%) for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng, as well as PassMark CPU and memory scores. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with small-block cached reads and large-block uncached writes ranking in the top 10%, while large-block uncached reads rank in the top 25%. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also fall within the top 10% tier. For LLM inference, the server achieves top-tier speeds for small and medium models, and strong (top 25%) performance for larger 70B models.

The primary hardware tradeoff of the E96as_v7 is the complete absence of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware, meaning workloads requiring local scratch space or hardware-accelerated parallel processing must rely on network-attached storage and CPU-based execution. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density of 8 GB per vCPU combined with top-tier compute performance provides a balanced resource profile for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of specialized GPU hardware.

This configuration is suited for memory-intensive applications, large-scale database operations, enterprise-grade web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference. It is also appropriate for data compression tasks, as demonstrated by its top-tier single-threaded and multi-threaded decompression benchmarks.
description: The Microsoft Azure E96as_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance from the Easv7 family, powered by a dedicated 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) and 768.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs but supports Premium Storage. In benchmark evaluations, the E96as_v7 delivers top-tier (top 10%) performance in single-core and multi-core CPU execution, database operations, and memory bandwidth. It also demonstrates strong performance in LLM inference for medium and large models. This resource configuration is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, large databases, enterprise web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference, offering high resource density and compute capabilities without the cost of specialized GPU hardware.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96as_v7 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC processor, delivering top-tier database and CPU benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96as_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC CPU, built for database workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized virtual machine featuring ninety-six vCPUs and top-tier database, memory bandwidth, and compute benchmark performance.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 768 GB RAM powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor
Top-tier performance in multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmarks
No local storage or GPU hardware, requiring network-attached storage
Strong CPU-based LLM inference capabilities for small and medium models
Suited for memory-intensive databases, enterprise web serving, and data compression
